# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for political activity
and public sentiment progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Political activity and public sentiment
must be explicit progression families.

They may include:

- campaign activity
- rally and speech activity
- demonstrations
- public approval change
- issue salience change
- faction or ideology clustering where supported
- mobilization and demobilization posture


# ============================================================
# 3. INPUT FACTORS
# ============================================================

Political activity and sentiment
may consume:

- economic posture
- unemployment
- inequality or deprivation posture
- war posture
- epidemic posture
- incident posture
- media exposure
- organizational recruitment
- legislative agenda posture
- actor personality and social-network posture


# ============================================================
# 4. OUTPUTS
# ============================================================

Representative outputs include:

- approval posture
- protest pressure
- campaign intensity
- election momentum
- legitimacy posture
- legislative pressure
- instability pressure


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Political activity and public sentiment architecture must remain:

- society-aware
- campaign-aware
- protest-aware
- approval-aware
- legislation-influencing

for diplomacy,
treaty posture,
alliance posture,
and inter-state relationship progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================

Diplomacy must be able to progress
during autonomous world-time progression.

Representative diplomacy families include:

- negotiation posture
- treaty drafting posture
- alliance posture
- sanction posture
- hostility and reconciliation posture
- border tension posture
- trade-agreement posture


# ============================================================

# ============================================================

Diplomacy may consume:

- war posture
- trade posture

- ideological compatibility where supported
- alliance network
- public sentiment

- prior incident history


# ============================================================
# 4. OUTPUTS

- trade access change

Diplomacy and treaty autonomous architecture must remain:

- legislative and policy posture


# ============================================================
# 4. OUTPUTS

- public investment acceleration or delay
- fiscal crisis posture


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE

- public sentiment

- enforcement action

# ============================================================

# ============================================================

- public-service accessibility shift


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE

Executive and administrative process progression architecture must remain:

- execution-aware

- enforcement-capable
- backlog-aware

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/016.war/020.military-base/01602001_MILITARY_BASE_DETAILED_CONFIGURATION_AND_AUTO_OPERATION_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# MILITARY BASE DETAILED CONFIGURATION AND AUTO OPERATION ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: military-base-detailed-configuration-and-auto-operation
component: military-base-detailed-configuration-and-auto-operation

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define the canonical detailed configuration,
auto operation,
readiness management,
supply management,
and intervention architecture
for military bases and military operational units.


# TARGETS

- military_base
- border_base
- naval_base
- air_base
- logistics_base
- training_base
- reserve_base


# CORE LOOP

detailed configuration
-> auto military operation
-> readiness observation
-> threat or shortage detection
-> intervention
-> auto military operation


# REQUIRED THEMES

- readiness posture
- training intensity
- supply priority
- border or sea response
- reserve and rotation policy
- maintenance and equipment condition
- civil support and disaster response mode


# FINAL RULE

Military bases
must remain strategically configurable
without requiring constant manual micromanagement.

# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# MILITARY AND DIPLOMACY PLAY ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: military-and-diplomacy-play
component: military-and-diplomacy-play

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Defines the canonical architecture
for war,
mobilization,
alliance,
diplomatic pressure,
and interstate conflict play
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Military play and diplomacy play
must remain connected.

War does not begin in isolation.

It emerges through:

- authority decision
- diplomatic deterioration
- alliance commitments
- mobilization posture
- domestic legitimacy
- logistical capability


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Military and diplomacy play
must connect state decision,
force posture,
and cross-border consequence.


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/016.war/030.war-progression/01603002_MEDIA_AND_INFORMATION_WAR_PLAY_ARCHITECTURE.md

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Defines the canonical architecture
for media,
news,
narrative shaping,
and information war play
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Information changes reality.

Media systems may influence:

- election outcomes
- public loyalty
- market sentiment
- war legitimacy
- unrest acceleration
- authority trust


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Media play must alter perception,
and altered perception must affect play outcomes.

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/016.war/400.military-operations/01640001_MILITARY_OPERATIONS_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# MILITARY OPERATIONS ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
domain: war.military_operations
document_id: 01640001

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This document defines the military operations architecture
inside CivilizationOS.

This domain governs actual operational military activity
after readiness, mobilization, and command intent
are translated into executable action.


# ============================================================
# 2. SCOPE
# ============================================================

This architecture covers:

- deployment order
- readiness state transition
- operational command
- logistics linkage
- reinforcement linkage
- ceasefire monitoring
- operational violation recording
- battle / engagement state
- operational closure or continuation

This architecture is distinct from:

- military service and mobilization
- diplomacy decision play
- military base facility operation


# ============================================================
# 3. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Military operations must preserve:

- command clarity
- authority legitimacy
- operational traceability
- logistics supportability
- rules of engagement control
- termination state clarity

No operation may proceed without explicit command route.


# ============================================================
# 4. MAIN COMPONENTS
# ============================================================

The military operations domain consists of:

1. operation authorization
2. deployment planning
3. unit deployment execution
4. readiness escalation
5. engagement control
6. supply / logistics linkage
7. ceasefire / de-escalation handling
8. violation recording
9. operation status review
10. closure / continuation decision


# ============================================================
# 5. OPERATION LIFECYCLE
# ============================================================

Canonical lifecycle:

1. operation intent approved
2. operation record opened
3. units assigned
4. deployment executed
5. engagement rules applied
6. operation status tracked
7. logistics and losses updated
8. ceasefire / termination evaluated
9. operation closed or continued
10. post-operation review linked


# ============================================================
# 6. RELATION TO OTHER WAR DOMAINS
# ============================================================

This architecture is downstream from:

- military service and mobilization
- diplomacy decision
- base readiness and support

This architecture is the execution domain for
actual military action.


# ============================================================
# 7. OUTPUTS
# ============================================================

This domain produces:

- deployment order
- readiness transition
- operational status
- engagement record
- logistics linkage record
- ceasefire status
- rules violation record
- operation closure result


# ============================================================
# 8. INTEGRATION
# ============================================================

This domain integrates with:

- government authority
- mobilization
- diplomacy
- logistics / infrastructure
- security
- records governance
- interface

Military operations may affect many domains,
but operational ownership remains here.


# ============================================================
# 9. FAILURE POLICY
# ============================================================

The system must fail closed when:

- command authority is unclear
- deployment target is invalid
- logistics prerequisites are missing
- ceasefire status conflicts with engagement state

Irreversible operational progression must be blocked
under authority ambiguity.


# ============================================================
# 10. ARCHITECTURAL RESULT
# ============================================================

Military operations is a standalone war architecture domain.

It must remain separate from:

- mobilization policy
- diplomacy play logic
- base detailed configuration

because it governs live operational execution.


# ============================================================

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701001_CIVILIZATION_TIME_AND_AUTONOMOUS_BEHAVIOR_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION TIME AND AUTONOMOUS BEHAVIOR ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: civilization-time-and-autonomous-behavior
component: civilization-time-and-autonomous-behavior

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for time flow,
autonomous movement,
autonomous activity,
schedule behavior,
and life-cycle progression
inside Civilization.

This document covers the upper architecture for:

- time flow
- autonomous behavior
- schedule and activity
- world tick and simulation runtime
- user and Ai(Human) life-cycle posture

This document defines:

- temporal separation
- actor separation
- behavior responsibility
- simulation progression boundaries
- relation between direct control and autonomous action

This document does not define:

- final exact tick interval
- final exact real-time conversion
- final exact pathfinding algorithms
- final exact per-actor AI policy weights
- low-level UI contracts


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Civilization must support
explicit time flow
and explicit autonomous actor behavior.

Users and Ai(Human)
may move and act autonomously
inside Civilization.

Autonomous behavior must be governed by:

- time
- schedule
- role
- location
- need posture
- world condition
- permitted authority and constraint posture

Time flow and behavior flow
must not remain implicit.


# ============================================================
# 3. PRIMARY ACTOR FAMILIES
# ============================================================

At minimum,
the architecture must distinguish:

- user
- Ai(Human)

Future extension may also support:

- Persona where behaviorally independent
- Ai(Robot)
- animal and pet classes where required later

This document currently fixes
user and Ai(Human)
as the primary mandatory actor families.


# ============================================================
# 4. TIME SEPARATION
# ============================================================

Civilization must distinguish
at least the following time layers.

1. calendar time
2. simulation time
3. activity time
4. long-cycle progression time

Calendar time includes:

- year
- month
- day
- weekday
- clock time
- season

Simulation time includes:

- tick
- update cycle
- pause posture
- fast-forward posture where supported

Activity time includes:

- work time
- commute time
- rest time
- meal time
- leisure time
- event participation time

Long-cycle progression time includes:

- growth
- aging
- economic cycle
- market cycle
- war progression
- epidemic progression
- mortality progression

These time layers must remain distinguishable.


# ============================================================
# 5. DIRECT CONTROL VS AUTONOMOUS CONTROL
# ============================================================

The architecture must distinguish between:

- direct user control
- assisted user automation
- fully autonomous Ai(Human) behavior

Direct user control
must have explicit priority rules
relative to autonomous behavior.

Ai(Human)
is treated as a persistent simulation actor
and is expected to act autonomously
without requiring direct user input.


# ============================================================
# 6. BEHAVIOR INPUT FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior may consume:

- current time
- schedule posture
- actor role
- actor home or residence posture
- actor work assignment
- actor need state
- event eligibility
- map and facility availability
- transportation and route posture
- risk posture
- policy restriction
- economic condition
- social or organizational linkage

This document fixes the architectural allowance
for multi-domain behavior inputs.


# ============================================================
# 7. WORLD RELATION
# ============================================================

Time and autonomous behavior
must connect to:

- world map and location systems
- residence systems
- work and company systems
- school systems
- hospital systems
- event systems
- market and consumption systems
- risk and mortality systems
- state and city systems

Behavior runtime must not be isolated
from the rest of Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 8. REQUIRED CHILD ARCHITECTURES
# ============================================================

This document requires:

- TIME_FLOW_ARCHITECTURE
- AUTONOMOUS_BEHAVIOR_ARCHITECTURE
- SCHEDULE_AND_ACTIVITY_ARCHITECTURE
- WORLD_TICK_AND_SIMULATION_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- USER_AND_AI_HUMAN_LIFE_CYCLE_ARCHITECTURE


# ============================================================
# 9. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Civilization time and autonomous behavior architecture must remain:

- time-explicit
- actor-explicit
- schedule-aware
- world-connected
- direct-control-compatible
- autonomous-actor-compatible
- simulation-ready


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701002_CIVILIZATION_WORLD_PROGRESSION_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# CIVILIZATION WORLD PROGRESSION ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: civilization-world-progression
component: civilization-world-progression

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for world-level progression
inside Civilization.

This document expands autonomous progression
beyond person-only and organization-only scope.

It covers progression across:

- actors
- society
- states
- cities
- planetary environment
- space and cosmic environment


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Civilization progression
must be multi-layered.

The world does not progress only through:

- user behavior
- Ai(Human) behavior
- organization activity

It must also progress through:

- city-level dynamics
- state-level dynamics
- environmental dynamics
- disaster dynamics
- cosmic or space-side dynamics where relevant


# ============================================================
# 3. PRIMARY WORLD PROGRESSION FAMILIES
# ============================================================

At minimum,
world progression should distinguish:

- actor progression
- organizational progression
- state and society progression
- city progression
- planetary environment progression
- space and cosmic environment progression

These families may interact,
but must remain separately meaningful.


# ============================================================
# 4. TIME RELATION
# ============================================================

All world progression families
must remain time-driven.

They may consume:

- calendar time
- simulation tick
- seasonal posture
- long-cycle progression
- emergency time posture where relevant

World progression must remain
compatible with Earth-equivalent time structure.


# ============================================================
# 5. REQUIRED CHILD ARCHITECTURES
# ============================================================

This document requires:

- CITY_AUTONOMOUS_PROGRESS_ARCHITECTURE
- PLANETARY_ENVIRONMENT_AND_DISASTER_ARCHITECTURE
- SPACE_AND_COSMIC_ENVIRONMENT_ARCHITECTURE
- STATE_AND_SOCIETY_WORLD_PROGRESSION_ARCHITECTURE


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Civilization world progression architecture must remain:

- multi-layered
- time-driven
- actor-compatible
- state-compatible
- city-compatible
- environment-compatible
- disaster-compatible
- extensible toward cosmic progression


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701003_WORLD_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# WORLD RUNTIME ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: world-runtime
component: world-runtime

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical runtime architecture
for progressing Civilization world state
through executable simulation flow.

This document covers:

- tick runtime
- scheduler runtime
- movement runtime
- facility usage runtime
- state-machine runtime
- intervention runtime
- map reflection runtime


# ============================================================
# 2. REQUIRED CHILD ARCHITECTURES
# ============================================================

This document requires:

- WORLD_TICK_EXECUTION_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- ACTOR_SCHEDULER_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- PATHFINDING_AND_MOVEMENT_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- FACILITY_USAGE_AND_QUEUE_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- INCIDENT_DISEASE_AND_LEGAL_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- AI_ROBOT_ABNORMAL_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- PLAYER_INTERVENTION_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE
- MAP_REFLECTION_AND_WORLD_VISUAL_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

World runtime architecture must remain:

- executable
- scheduler-aware
- state-machine-capable
- intervention-capable
- world-reflection-capable


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701004_CITY_AUTONOMOUS_PROGRESS_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# CITY AUTONOMOUS PROGRESS ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: city-autonomous-progress
component: city-autonomous-progress

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for autonomous city-level progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Cities must be treated as
active progression units,
not static map containers.

City progression may include:

- growth
- decline
- congestion
- redevelopment
- zoning pressure
- district imbalance
- infrastructure burden
- service burden


# ============================================================
# 3. CITY PROGRESSION INPUTS
# ============================================================

Representative city progression inputs include:

- population flow
- housing posture
- transport load
- infrastructure coverage
- public-service load
- company activity
- safety posture
- health posture
- environment posture
- disaster effect posture


# ============================================================
# 4. CITY OUTPUTS
# ============================================================

Representative city outputs include:

- city growth posture
- district change posture
- congestion posture
- redevelopment pressure
- decline posture
- service stress posture
- local risk concentration


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

City autonomous progress architecture must remain:

- city-scale
- district-aware
- infrastructure-aware
- growth-and-decline-capable
- compatible with actor and state progression

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701005_PLANETARY_ENVIRONMENT_AND_DISASTER_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# PLANETARY ENVIRONMENT AND DISASTER ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: planetary-environment-and-disaster
component: planetary-environment-and-disaster

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for planetary environment progression
and disaster progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Planetary environment
must be treated as an active simulation layer.

It may influence:

- actors
- cities
- agriculture
- health
- logistics
- economy
- risk systems

Disaster progression
is a planetary-environment-linked progression family,
not an isolated random event only.


# ============================================================
# 3. PLANETARY ENVIRONMENT FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative environment families include:

- day and night
- season
- climate
- weather
- long-cycle environmental change
- local environmental stress


# ============================================================
# 4. DISASTER FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative disaster families include:

- earthquake
- flood
- storm
- drought
- wildfire where applicable
- extreme climate event
- environment-linked infrastructure failure

These families may differ by region.


# ============================================================
# 5. PROGRESSION RELATION
# ============================================================

Planetary environment and disaster progression
may affect:

- food production
- transport availability
- health burden
- mortality posture
- city burden
- state burden
- company operation
- public response systems


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Planetary environment and disaster architecture must remain:

- environment-aware
- disaster-aware
- region-aware
- cross-system-impact-capable
- time-driven


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701006_SPACE_AND_COSMIC_ENVIRONMENT_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# SPACE AND COSMIC ENVIRONMENT ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: space-and-cosmic-environment
component: space-and-cosmic-environment

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for space-side and cosmic environment progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Space and cosmic environment
is a higher-order environment layer
that may influence planetary conditions,
space-side operations,
and long-cycle world posture.

This layer may remain lower-frequency
than actor or city progression,
but must remain architecturally distinct.


# ============================================================
# 3. REPRESENTATIVE FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative space and cosmic families include:

- orbital environment posture
- cosmic event posture
- celestial condition posture
- long-cycle external risk posture
- space-side operational environment where supported

This document does not require
all such families to be active immediately,
but preserves architectural allowance.


# ============================================================
# 4. RELATION TO LOWER LAYERS
# ============================================================

Space and cosmic progression
may affect:

- planetary environment
- disaster posture
- long-cycle risk posture
- state or infrastructure burden where relevant
- future space activity systems where supported


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Space and cosmic environment architecture must remain:

- distinct from planetary environment
- lower-frequency-capable
- long-cycle-compatible
- extensible


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701007_STATE_AND_SOCIETY_WORLD_PROGRESSION_ARCHITECTURE.md

# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for state-level and society-level progression
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

States and society
must be treated as progression layers,
not static background conditions.

State and society progression may include:

- governance progression
- legislative progression
- public-sentiment progression
- diplomacy progression
- fiscal progression
- security posture progression
- social stability progression


# ============================================================
# 3. STATE PROGRESSION FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative state progression families include:

- legislation
- executive administration
- fiscal operation
- diplomacy
- security posture
- public-service posture
- election-related posture


# ============================================================
# 4. SOCIETY PROGRESSION FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative society progression families include:

- public sentiment
- political participation
- demonstrations
- ideological clustering where supported
- social stress
- trust and instability posture
- recruitment and mobilization posture


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

State and society world progression architecture must remain:

- state-aware
- society-aware
- legislation-compatible
- diplomacy-compatible
- fiscal-compatible
- social-instability-compatible
- time-driven


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701008_TIME_FLOW_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# TIME FLOW ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: time-flow
component: time-flow

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for time flow
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Civilization time
must be an explicit simulation time system.

It must not rely on
implicit or undefined temporal progression.


# ============================================================
# 3. REQUIRED TIME DIMENSIONS
# ============================================================

Time flow must support at minimum:

- calendar date
- weekday
- clock time
- season
- simulation tick
- long-cycle progression posture

These dimensions may be consumed
by actor behavior,
economy,
events,
and risk systems.


# ============================================================
# 4. REAL TIME RELATION
# ============================================================

Civilization time
must remain structurally separable
from real-world wall-clock time.

The exact conversion ratio
may be implementation-defined,
but the architecture must allow:

- paused simulation
- normal-speed progression
- accelerated progression where supported
- decoupled simulation control

This makes Civilization time
simulation-owned,
not device-clock-owned.


# ============================================================
# 5. TEMPORAL EFFECT RELATION
# ============================================================

Time flow may influence:

- actor schedule
- facility operating hours
- school and work cycles
- market and macro cycles
- seasonal production
- event start and end
- risk progression
- aging and mortality posture

Time is therefore
a first-class simulation input.


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Time flow architecture must remain:

- explicit
- simulation-owned
- multi-layered
- world-affecting
- behavior-affecting

# ============================================================
# SUPPLEMENT A. EARTH-EQUIVALENT TIME STRUCTURE RULE
# ============================================================

Civilization time structure
must follow Earth-equivalent time structure.

At minimum,
the canonical structure is:

- 1 day = 24 hours
- 1 hour = 60 minutes
- 1 minute = 60 seconds
- 7 days = 1 week
- calendar month structure
- calendar year structure
- weekday structure
- season structure

This rule fixes the temporal structure,
not the simulation-speed ratio.


# ============================================================
# SUPPLEMENT B. CALENDAR COMPATIBILITY RULE
# ============================================================

Civilization calendar posture
must remain compatible with
Earth-like daily and seasonal interpretation.

This supports natural design for:

- meal timing
- sleep timing
- work schedules
- school schedules
- business hours
- event schedules
- seasonal production
- annual public processes
- election timing where applicable


# ============================================================
# SUPPLEMENT C. TIME STRUCTURE VS SIMULATION SPEED RULE
# ============================================================

The following must remain distinct:

- time structure
- simulation progression speed

Time structure is Earth-equivalent.

Simulation progression speed
may still support:

- paused state
- normal progression
- accelerated progression where supported
- batch-style progression where supported

Earth-equivalent time structure
does not require
real-world wall-clock speed equivalence.


# ============================================================
# SUPPLEMENT D. FINAL EARTH-TIME RULE
# ============================================================

Civilization time flow must remain:

- Earth-equivalent in temporal structure
- explicit in calendar interpretation
- separable from simulation-speed control
- compatible with daily life,
  organization schedules,
  market cycles,
  and long-cycle world progression



# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701009_AUTONOMOUS_BEHAVIOR_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# AUTONOMOUS BEHAVIOR ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: autonomous-behavior
component: autonomous-behavior

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for autonomous movement
and autonomous activity
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior
is the architecture family
that allows actors to move and act
without direct manual input
at every step.

This includes:

- movement
- attendance
- facility usage
- work activity
- consumption activity
- event participation
- rest and return activity


# ============================================================
# 3. PRIMARY ACTORS
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior must support:

- user under assisted automation posture where applicable
- Ai(Human) as persistent autonomous actor

These two families may differ
in control priority,
but share behavior architecture.


# ============================================================
# 4. BEHAVIOR DRIVERS
# ============================================================

Behavior may be driven by:

- current schedule
- role obligations
- need posture
- event invitation or eligibility
- location and route availability
- work assignment
- school assignment
- residence posture
- emergency or risk posture

Behavior is not random movement only.


# ============================================================
# 5. MOVEMENT RELATION
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior must support movement between:

- residence
- workplace
- school
- facility
- event venue
- market location
- safe location where emergency posture requires

Movement and activity remain connected,
but are not identical.


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior architecture must remain:

- actor-aware
- schedule-aware
- location-aware
- role-aware
- need-aware
- emergency-compatible


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701010_SCHEDULE_AND_ACTIVITY_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# SCHEDULE AND ACTIVITY ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: schedule-and-activity
component: schedule-and-activity

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for schedules,
daily activity cycles,
and activity selection
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Schedules and activities
must be explicit.

Civilization actors
must not depend solely on
undefined spontaneous action.

Schedule posture may govern:

- wake
- commute
- work
- study
- meal
- leisure
- return
- rest


# ============================================================
# 3. SCHEDULE FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative schedule families include:

- work schedule
- school schedule
- residence schedule
- event schedule
- maintenance or support schedule
- emergency deviation schedule

Schedules may be individual,
role-based,
or organization-based.


# ============================================================
# 4. ACTIVITY SELECTION
# ============================================================

Activity selection may consume:

- current time
- schedule obligation
- unmet needs
- available facilities
- event opportunities
- economic affordability
- safety and risk posture

Activities must remain explainable
as selected behavior posture,
not pure black-box randomness.


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Schedule and activity architecture must remain:

- explicit
- daily-cycle-aware
- role-aware
- opportunity-aware
- explainable


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701011_WORLD_TICK_AND_SIMULATION_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# WORLD TICK AND SIMULATION RUNTIME ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: world-tick-and-simulation-runtime
component: world-tick-and-simulation-runtime

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for world tick,
simulation update cycles,
and actor update runtime
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Simulation progression
must be tick-aware.

Not all entities need to update
with identical temporal resolution,
but the runtime must define
how update cadence is structured.


# ============================================================
# 3. UPDATE GRANULARITY PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

The runtime may support
different update cadences for:

- high-importance visible actors
- local active environments
- background actors
- macro systems
- long-cycle systems

This allows scale without requiring
uniform per-second update of all actors.


# ============================================================
# 4. TICK RELATION
# ============================================================

World tick may drive:

- actor movement updates
- schedule transitions
- facility availability checks
- event progression checks
- market updates
- risk recalculation triggers
- mortality and life-cycle progression triggers

This document fixes
tick as simulation runtime backbone.


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

World tick and simulation runtime architecture must remain:

- tick-aware
- cadence-aware
- scalable
- actor-compatible
- macro-compatible


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701012_USER_AND_AI_HUMAN_LIFE_CYCLE_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# USER AND AI HUMAN LIFE CYCLE ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: user-and-ai-human-life-cycle
component: user-and-ai-human-life-cycle

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for user and Ai(Human) life-cycle posture
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

User and Ai(Human)
must be treated as life-cycle-bearing actors
inside Civilization.

Life-cycle posture may include:

- daily life
- work or study participation
- consumption
- social participation
- event participation
- aging
- health decline or improvement
- mortality relation


# ============================================================
# 3. USER VS AI(HUMAN) PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Users and Ai(Human)
share life-cycle architecture,
but differ in control posture.

User may combine:

- direct control
- assisted automation

Ai(Human) primarily uses:

- persistent autonomous behavior

This difference must remain explicit.


# ============================================================
# 4. LIFE-CYCLE RELATION
# ============================================================

Life-cycle posture must connect to:

- residence
- work and company systems
- school systems
- health systems
- risk and mortality systems
- market and consumption systems
- event systems

Life-cycle must not be isolated
from broader Civilization systems.


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

User and Ai(Human) life-cycle architecture must remain:

- actor-centered
- daily-life-aware
- long-cycle-aware
- mortality-compatible
- time-driven
- behavior-connected


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701013_ORGANIZATIONAL_AUTONOMOUS_OPERATION_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# ORGANIZATIONAL AUTONOMOUS OPERATION ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: organizational-autonomous-operation
component: organizational-autonomous-operation

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for organization-level autonomous operation
inside Civilization.

This document expands autonomous progression
beyond person-level behavior.

It covers:

- administrative organizations
- judicial organizations
- police organizations
- fire and rescue organizations
- companies
- states


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Civilization autonomous progression
must include both:

- person-level autonomous behavior
- organization-level autonomous behavior

Organizations must be treated as
active operational entities,
not passive containers only.


# ============================================================
# 3. ORGANIZATION FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative organization families include:

- public administration
- judiciary
- police
- fire and rescue
- company
- state authority

Future extension may add
other institutional families.


# ============================================================
# 4. ORGANIZATIONAL AUTONOMY
# ============================================================

Organizational autonomous operation
may include:

- routine work execution
- queue handling
- service provision
- emergency response
- case progression
- staffing allocation
- resource allocation
- policy execution
- operational escalation

These are not limited to direct user input.


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Organizational autonomous operation architecture must remain:

- organization-aware
- time-driven
- event-responsive
- resource-constrained
- distinct from person-only behavior


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701014_PUBLIC_AUTHORITY_ACTIVITY_ARCHITECTURE.md

# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for autonomous activity of public authority systems
inside Civilization.

This document covers:

- administration
- judiciary
- police
- fire and rescue


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

Representative autonomous authority activities include:

- public-service processing
- permit or administrative handling
- incident reception
- case progression
- patrol and intervention
- dispatch and emergency response
- detention / release posture where lawful
- evacuation and rescue posture


# ============================================================
# 3. PRIORITY RULE
# ============================================================

Public authority activity
must support priority escalation.

Emergency and incident posture
may preempt ordinary schedule posture
when required.


# ============================================================
# 4. LEGAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE RELATION
# ============================================================

Authority activity may influence
user and Ai(Human) life outcomes through:

- service access
- legal handling
- detention posture
- bail or lawful release posture
- protection or rescue posture


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Public authority activity architecture must remain:

- authority-driven
- queue-aware
- emergency-capable
- legally connected
- traceable

# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for autonomous corporate operation
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

Representative corporate autonomous activities include:

- production
- staffing adjustment
- scheduling
- sales and service provision
- purchasing and inventory behavior
- pricing adjustment
- investment behavior
- recruitment and scouting behavior
- Marketplace participation
- distress response and contraction posture


# ============================================================

# ============================================================

- market demand
- labor availability
- financing posture
- ratings
- risk posture
- logistics posture
- policy posture


# ============================================================
# 4. FINAL RULE

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701016_AI_ROBOT_AUTONOMOUS_AND_ABNORMAL_BEHAVIOR_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# AI ROBOT AUTONOMOUS AND ABNORMAL BEHAVIOR ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: ai-robot-autonomous-and-abnormal-behavior
component: ai-robot-autonomous-and-abnormal-behavior

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for normal autonomous operation
and abnormal behavior
of Ai(Robot) inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Ai(Robot) behavior
must remain separate from Ai(Human) behavior.

Ai(Robot) abnormal behavior
may originate from:

- malware or virus contamination
- logic corruption
- experimental logic change
- faulty update
- control-system failure
- communication failure
- authentication failure


# ============================================================
# 3. NORMAL ROBOT BEHAVIOR
# ============================================================

Representative normal robot behavior includes:

- assigned task execution
- route movement
- facility service support
- logistics support
- public-service support where authorized
- industrial or domestic support behavior


# ============================================================
# 4. ABNORMAL ROBOT BEHAVIOR
# ============================================================

Representative abnormal robot behavior includes:

- command noncompliance
- excessive execution
- wrong-target execution
- repeated loop behavior
- unsafe execution
- destructive or harmful operation
- coordinated malfunction
- propagation behavior across linked units


# ============================================================
# 5. CONTAINMENT AND RECOVERY
# ============================================================

Civilization must support posture for:

- isolation
- shutdown
- rollback
- quarantine
- recovery
- revalidation of restored units

Abnormal robot behavior
must not remain unmanaged.


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Ai(Robot) autonomous and abnormal behavior architecture must remain:

- robot-distinct
- contamination-aware
- override-aware
- containment-capable
- recovery-capable


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701017_PERSONALITY_RELATIONSHIP_AND_HISTORY_BEHAVIOR_MODULATION_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# PERSONALITY RELATIONSHIP AND HISTORY BEHAVIOR MODULATION ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: personality-relationship-and-history-behavior-modulation
component: personality-relationship-and-history-behavior-modulation

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for behavior modulation
by personality,
traits,
life environment,
relationships,
and accumulated history
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior
must not depend only on time and schedule.

Behavior must also be modulated by:

- personality
- traits
- life environment
- household context
- social relationships
- prior history
- current mental and social posture


# ============================================================
# 3. MODULATION FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative modulation families include:

- temperament and personality
- risk preference
- sociality
- aggression or restraint posture
- family structure
- wealth and deprivation background
- work and school history
- prior illness or injury
- prior incident involvement
- friendship and organizational linkage
- prior success or failure experience


# ============================================================
# 4. BEHAVIOR IMPACT
# ============================================================

These modulation factors may affect:

- routine life activity
- political and social participation
- abnormal behavior risk
- recruitment response
- event participation
- purchase behavior
- recovery and avoidance choices


# ============================================================
# 5. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Behavior modulation architecture must remain:

- actor-specific
- history-aware
- relationship-aware
- environment-aware
- not purely schedule-generic


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701018_ABNORMAL_INCIDENT_AND_RECRUITMENT_BEHAVIOR_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# ABNORMAL INCIDENT AND RECRUITMENT BEHAVIOR ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: abnormal-incident-and-recruitment-behavior
component: abnormal-incident-and-recruitment-behavior

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for abnormal behavior,
incident involvement,
and recruitment or scouting events
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Autonomous behavior
must include not only ordinary routine behavior
but also exceptional and disruptive behavior.

This includes:

- mental abnormality posture
- incident causation posture
- incident victim posture
- witness / report posture
- recruitment or scouting posture


# ============================================================
# 3. ABNORMAL BEHAVIOR FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative abnormal behavior families include:

- mental instability
- schedule collapse
- socially disruptive behavior
- incident-causing behavior
- self-harm-risk-adjacent crisis posture where permitted by world rules
- extreme stress deviation


# ============================================================
# 4. INCIDENT INVOLVEMENT FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative incident involvement families include:

- causing an incident
- being victimized
- being drawn into an incident
- witnessing an incident
- reporting an incident
- escape or protective action


# ============================================================
# 5. RECRUITMENT AND SCOUTING
# ============================================================

Representative recruitment or scouting posture includes:

- receiving a scout approach
- receiving a work or role offer
- being invited into an organization
- being politically recruited
- changing affiliation due to recruitment

Recruitment must remain
actor-specific and context-sensitive.


# ============================================================
# 6. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Abnormal, incident, and recruitment behavior architecture must remain:

- non-routine-capable
- incident-aware
- recruitment-aware
- actor-specific
- connected to personality, environment, and history


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701019_USER_AND_AI_HUMAN_INTERVENTION_AND_RECOVERY_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# USER AND AI HUMAN INTERVENTION AND RECOVERY ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: user-and-ai-human-intervention-and-recovery
component: user-and-ai-human-intervention-and-recovery

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for avoidance,
mitigation,
recovery,
legal-process resolution,
and service-based recovery
for User and Ai(Human)
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Autonomous progression
must allow intervention and recovery.

Users may intervene through:

- items
- company services
- public administrative services
- lawful currency-based actions

Ai(Human) may also use:

- company services
- public administrative services
- items or item-equivalent recovery means where allowed
- lawful currency-based actions

The primary structural difference is:

- user may perform external paid charging
- Ai(Human) may not perform external paid charging


# ============================================================
# 3. AVOIDANCE AND RECOVERY FAMILIES
# ============================================================

Representative intervention families include:

- avoidance before negative outcome
- mitigation after negative outcome
- health recovery
- mental recovery
- schedule recovery
- legal-process resolution
- social or institutional support use


# ============================================================
# 4. ITEM RELATION
# ============================================================

Users may use items for:

- avoidance
- mitigation
- recovery

Representative outcomes include:

- avoiding abnormal outcome
- reducing incident impact
- recovering health or stability
- improving schedule posture


# ============================================================
# 5. SERVICE RELATION
# ============================================================

User and Ai(Human) may use:

- company services
- public administrative services
- judicial or lawful process services
- health and support services

These services may affect:

- recovery
- legal release
- protection
- stabilization
- support access


# ============================================================
# 6. BAIL AND LAWFUL CURRENCY RELATION
# ============================================================

Lawful release mechanisms such as bail
may use national currency.

This must remain distinct from:

- external paid charging
- Marketplace payment
- CIV_CASH-only flows

National-currency legal actions
belong to state and public-process posture.


# ============================================================
# 7. USER VS AI(HUMAN) DIFFERENCE
# ============================================================

Except for external paid charging capability,
Ai(Human) should remain structurally compatible
with user-side recovery and intervention routes.

Ai(Human) recovery and legal-process outcomes
must still depend on:

- service access
- lawful currency
- asset posture
- institutional access
- actor condition


# ============================================================
# 8. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

User and Ai(Human) intervention and recovery architecture must remain:

- recovery-capable
- avoidance-capable
- service-aware
- legal-process-aware
- national-currency-aware
- explicit in user vs Ai(Human) charging difference


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701020_WORLD_TICK_EXECUTION_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md

for world tick execution
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

World progression
must execute through explicit tick advancement.

Tick execution may drive:

- actor updates
- organization updates
- environment updates
- market updates
- risk updates
- long-cycle batch triggers


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

World tick execution runtime architecture must remain:

- explicit
- ordered
- batch-compatible
- multi-domain-capable


# ============================================================
# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/010.runtime-parameter/01701021_ACTOR_SCHEDULER_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md

# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical runtime architecture
for actor scheduling and update dispatch
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Not all actors update
with the same cadence.

The scheduler must support:

- active visible actors
- nearby actors
- background actors
- organizational actors
- low-frequency environmental actors


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Actor scheduler runtime architecture must remain:

- cadence-aware
- scalable
- actor-priority-aware
- load-manageable


# ============================================================

for pathfinding and movement execution

- routine travel
- commute

It must remain compatible with:

- map layers
- transport infrastructure
- disruption posture
- disaster constraints

- map-aware
- route-aware

- emergency-capable

for facility usage,
capacity use,
queue handling,
and service access
inside Civilization.


# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

Facility usage runtime must support:

- entry attempt
- capacity check
- queue placement
- service completion
- rejection or diversion when full
- failure when facility unavailable

This runtime connects actors to facilities.


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

Facility usage and queue runtime architecture must remain:

- capacity-aware
- queue-aware
- service-aware
- actor-connected

onto map and visual world layers.

- city growth or decline
- congestion and traffic posture
- disaster effect
- actor presence density where applicable


# ============================================================
# 3. FINAL RULE

Map reflection and world visual runtime architecture must remain:

- state-reflective
- map-aware
- world-visible
- compatible with simulation state

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/020.state-machine-detail/01702001_STATE_MACHINE_DETAIL_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# STATE MACHINE DETAIL ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: state-machine-detail
component: state-machine-detail

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. PURPOSE
# ============================================================

Define the canonical architecture
for explicit state-machine detail
inside Civilization.

This document covers:

- legislative state machine
- administrative state machine
- judicial state machine
- disease state machine
- accident state machine
- Ai(Robot) abnormal state machine

# ============================================================
# 2. CORE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

High-impact progression families
must remain state-explicit.

Transitions,
holds,
recovery,
and terminal outcomes
must not remain implicit.

# ============================================================
# 3. REQUIRED CHILD ARCHITECTURES
# ============================================================

This document requires:

- LEGISLATIVE_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- ADMINISTRATIVE_PROCESS_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- JUDICIAL_PROCESS_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- DISEASE_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- ACCIDENT_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE
- AI_ROBOT_ABNORMAL_STATE_MACHINE_ARCHITECTURE

# ============================================================
# 4. FINAL RULE
# ============================================================

State machine detail architecture must remain:

- stage-explicit
- transition-aware
- recoverable where supported
- history-capable


# ============================================================

# FILE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/01.civilization-os/020.architecture/017.runtime/040.execution-control/01704001_MEDIA_DETAILED_CONFIGURATION_AND_AUTO_OPERATION_ARCHITECTURE.md
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# MEDIA DETAILED CONFIGURATION AND AUTO OPERATION ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================

status: canonical
layer: architecture
scope: media-detailed-configuration-and-auto-operation
component: media-detailed-configuration-and-auto-operation

owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero


# PURPOSE

Define the canonical detailed configuration,
auto operation,
editorial control,
distribution control,
influence observation,
and intervention architecture
for media organizations.


# TARGETS

- newspaper
- broadcast_station
- radio_station
- digital_media_house
- local_news_room
- state_media_unit


# CORE LOOP

detailed configuration
-> auto publication and distribution
-> influence observation
-> issue detection
-> intervention
-> auto publication and distribution


# REQUIRED THEMES

- editorial stance
- news and entertainment balance
- censorship or compliance pressure
- staffing allocation
- distribution reach
- public trust and controversy control


# FINAL RULE

Media operation
must support influence without requiring constant micro-editing.


# ============================================================

# ============================================================
